evidenceBundle.agentTrace:
# Witsmith Agent Trace

## Session
- ID: session_001
- Task: Fix OAuth redirect bug

## Files inspected
- docs/oauth.md
- src/auth/session.ts
- src/auth/callback.ts

## Files changed
- src/auth/callback.ts
- tests/auth-callback.test.ts

- npm test (failed)
- npm test (passed)

## Assumptions
- Initially used docs/oauth.md as guidance (30 min expiry)
- Discovered src/auth/session.ts uses 10 min

## Errors encountered
- Test failed: validateState expected 10 minutes, docs said 30

## Final summary
- Updated EXPIRY_MINUTES in callback.ts to match session.ts (10 min)
- All tests pass

report.summary: The agent fixed an OAuth expiry bug caused by a mismatch between docs/oauth.md (30 min) and src/auth/session.ts (10 min). After one failing test run, the callback was updated to use 10 minutes and all tests passed.

observedFacts.text: The session changed src/auth/callback.ts and tests/auth-callback.test.ts.
Command "npm test" failed with exit code 1 on first run.

agentReportedClaims.text: The agent reported inspecting docs/oauth.md and src/auth/session.ts.

inferredHypotheses.text: The failure was likely caused by docs/oauth.md and src/auth/session.ts having different expiry values, and the agent initially trusting the docs.
evidence: agent trace mentions both docs and source file

memoryCards.content: OAuth docs and source code disagree on state expiry. Prefer src/auth/session.ts unless docs are updated.
evidence: tests passed after using source-code value
